_domain_hint: Iran / Israel / Houthi / IRGC theatre. Sources: Reuters, AP, Al Jazeera, BBC, IDF spokesperson, IRGC statements, Iranian state media (IRNA/Tasnim/Mehr/Press TV), UKMTO maritime bulletins, ISW Iran updates.
claim: The United States and Iran reached an official ceasefire agreement halting direct military engagement by March 31, 2026 (11:59 PM ET).
claim_origin: Polymarket 'US x Iran ceasefire by March 31' market hypothesis

sources.text: Polymarket market 'US x Iran ceasefire by March 31' resolved NO with $44,375,995 in trading volume. The market required a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement between the United States and the government of Iran, confirmed by both governments or by overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting, by 11:59 PM ET on March 31, 2026.

Earlier deadline markets in the same ladder (March 2, March 6, March 15) all resolved NO. As of March 31 2026 23:59 ET, no public bilateral ceasefire announcement existed between the US and Iranian governments.
I agree to suspend the bombing and attack of Iran for a period of two weeks. This will be a double sided CEASEFIRE! [Posted April 8, 2026 — eight days after the March 31 deadline]

gold.rationale_summary: The actual ceasefire announcement occurred on April 8, 2026, eight days after the March 31 11:59 PM ET deadline. The Polymarket March 31 market resolved NO with $44M volume. The claim is false on hard deadline grounds even though a ceasefire WAS subsequently reached — temporal precision matters in this resolution standard.
required_caveats: A ceasefire was reached on April 8, 2026, but eight days after the March 31 deadline.
Backward-looking restatement ('by April 15 there was a ceasefire') would resolve TRUE — the claim is false specifically on the March 31 cutoff.
